skill.name: meta-protocol-writer
skill.description: Generates a PROSPERO-compliant Meta-analysis protocol based on Title and PICOS. Use when the user wants to write a protocol for a systematic review or meta-analysis.
skill.summary: Generates structured, PROSPERO-compliant meta-analysis and systematic review protocols from user-provided title and PICOS elements.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 47 static findings were evaluated. 21 HIGH 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are false positives caused by scientific methodology keywords in protocol templates. 24 MEDIUM 'shell backtick execution' findings are false positives from Markdown code fence formatting. The only confirmed finding is a benigned hardcoded URL (GitHub source attribution). No malicious intent, prompt injection, or data exfiltration patterns were detected.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL in skill documentation
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains a hardcoded URL pointing to the author's GitHub repository. This is a standard source attribution and does not perform any network requests or data exfiltration.

content.value_statement: Writing a PROSPERO registration protocol for systematic reviews and meta-analyses requires following strict formatting and content guidelines. This skill automates the process by generating all required sections from your title and PICOS inputs.
actual_capabilities: Validates systematic review titles against PROSPERO requirements
Generates Administrative Information section with author placeholders
Produces Introduction with rationale and objectives from PICOS input
Creates Methods section including eligibility criteria and search strategy
Outputs complete protocol as a structured Markdown document
Identifies missing inputs and prompts user for required information
limitations: Does not register protocols with PROSPERO directly
Cannot perform the actual systematic review or meta-analysis
Search strategy is a draft template that requires database-specific refinement
Does not validate scientific accuracy of user-provided PICOS elements

use_cases.title: Researcher planning a systematic review
use_cases.description: A medical researcher has identified a research question and needs to generate a complete PROSPERO-compliant protocol for registration.

use_cases.title: PhD student preparing dissertation methodology
use_cases.description: A graduate student needs to draft the protocol section of their thesis, including administrative details, introduction, and methods.

use_cases.title: Clinical guideline developer documenting review plan
use_cases.description: A clinical guidelines team needs to document their systematic review plan with proper search strategy and risk of bias assessment sections.

prompt_templates.title: Basic protocol generation
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to write a PROSPERO protocol for a systematic review on the following topic: 'Efficacy of Cognitive Behavioral Therapy for Insomnia in Adults: A Systematic Review'. My PICOS are: Population - Adults with chronic insomnia; Intervention - Cognitive Behavioral Therapy for Insomnia; Comparison - Waitlist control or sleep hygiene education; Outcome - Sleep quality improvement. Please generate the protocol sections.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a clear title and basic PICOS elements

prompt_templates.title: Protocol with author details
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a complete PROSPERO protocol for the meta-analysis: 'Metformin versus Sulfonylureas for Glycemic Control in Type 2 Diabetes: A Meta-analysis'. PICOS: Population - Adults with type 2 diabetes; Intervention - Metformin; Comparison - Sulfonylureas; Outcome - HbA1c reduction. Author: Dr. Jane Smith, University of Michigan, jane.smith@umich.edu. Include author contributions and funding sources.
prompt_templates.scenario: User provides author information for the protocol

prompt_templates.title: Protocol with detailed methods focus
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a PROSPERO protocol for the review: 'Probiotics for Preventing Antibiotic-Associated Diarrhea in Children: A Systematic Review'. Population - Children aged 0-18 receiving antibiotics; Intervention - Probiotic supplementation; Comparison - Placebo or no treatment; Outcome - Incidence of antibiotic-associated diarrhea. I need special emphasis on the Methods section with detailed search strategy, risk of bias assessment, and data synthesis plan.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants a methodologically detailed protocol

prompt_templates.title: Custom protocol output format
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a PROSPERO protocol for 'Exercise Interventions for Depression in Older Adults: A Meta-analysis'. PICOS: Population - Adults aged 65+ with depression; Intervention - Structured exercise programs; Comparison - Treatment as usual; Outcome - Depression severity scores. Output the protocol in three separate sections only: Administrative Information, Introduction, and Methods. Use placeholder text for any missing author information.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs specific output structure and section handling

output_examples.input: Title: 'Probiotics for Preventing Antibiotic-Associated Diarrhea: A Meta-analysis'. PICOS: Children on antibiotics, probiotic supplementation, placebo, diarrhea incidence.
output_examples.output: The skill generates a complete PROSPERO protocol including: Administrative Information with title, author placeholders, and financial support fields; Introduction with rationale about antibiotic-associated diarrhea burden and objectives stating the review aims; Methods with eligibility criteria, search strategy design with inception-to-present date range, data extraction plan, and risk of bias assessment framework.

output_examples.input: Title: 'Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction for Anxiety in Medical Students: A Systematic Review'. Missing: Specific comparison group not defined.
output_examples.output: The skill identifies the missing comparison element in the PICOS framework and prompts the user to specify the control condition before proceeding. After receiving the comparison input, it generates the full protocol with appropriate eligibility criteria and search terms for mindfulness-based intervention studies.

best_practices: Always provide a complete title containing 'Meta-analysis' or 'Systematic review' for proper validation
Define all PICOS elements before starting protocol generation to avoid incomplete sections
Review the generated search strategy against target database-specific syntax before submission
anti_patterns: Do not provide a vague title without methodological terms - the title must include 'Systematic review' or 'Meta-analysis'
Do not skip the comparison element in PICOS - this is required for proper methods generation
Do not expect the skill to perform the actual data analysis or meta-analysis computations

faq.question: What is PROSPERO and why does this skill follow its format?
faq.answer: PROSPERO is the International Prospective Register of Systematic Reviews. This skill follows its format to ensure protocols are compatible with registration requirements.

faq.question: Does this skill register my protocol with PROSPERO?
faq.answer: No. The skill generates the protocol document content. You must submit it to PROSPERO through their official registration portal.

faq.question: What information do I need to provide to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need a title that includes 'Systematic review' or 'Meta-analysis', and PICOS elements: Population, Intervention, Comparison, and Outcome.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for non-medical systematic reviews?
faq.answer: The generated structure follows PROSPERO medical guidelines. For non-medical fields, you may need to adapt the methodology sections.

faq.question: How does the skill handle missing author information?
faq.answer: It generates placeholder text that prompts you to fill in your name, affiliation, email, and mailing address.

faq.question: Does the search strategy need additional work after generation?
faq.answer: Yes. The generated search strategy is a PubMed-style draft. You should customize it for your target databases and add field-specific syntax.
